reforms.description: The federal government has implemented a series of funding initiatives aimed at enhancing research capacity in universities and improving infrastructure, with a significant allocation of C$ 900 million for the Canada Foundation for Innovation to create 2,000 Canada Research Chairs and C$ 160 million for five research centres in genomic science. These measures are designed to bolster productive capacity and innovation in priority areas such as health care and technology.
reforms.component_name: Canada Research Chairs and Genomic Science Centres
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The funding initiatives are expected to enhance research capabilities and infrastructure, leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding initiatives have been executed as part of the federal budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform involves significant funding allocations and structural changes to enhance research capacity, qualifying it as a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The latest Budget provides for funding for initiatives that, among other things, seek to boost universities’ research capacity, improve infrastructure...

reforms.description: The federal government has initiated the involvement of external partners in the planning, design, and delivery of its innovation programmes through Alternative Service Delivery (ASD) arrangements, aiming to enhance efficiency and spur innovative practices.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By involving external partners, the reform is expected to improve the efficiency of innovation programmes and foster innovative practices.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has begun to implement these arrangements.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in how innovation programmes are delivered, involving new partnerships.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has begun to more actively involve external partners in the planning, design and delivery of its programmes, or so-called Alternative Service Delivery (ASD) by forming collaborative agreements.

reforms.description: The Quebec government has introduced a ten-year tax holiday for job-creating investment projects, along with a refundable tax credit for e-commerce support aimed at stimulating economic development and innovation among SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentives are designed to encourage investment and innovation in the private sector, which is expected to boost economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax holiday and credit have been officially announced and are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of tax incentives represents a significant policy change aimed at fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Businesses will benefit from a ten-year tax holiday for job-creating investment projects, a refundable tax credit for e-commerce support for SMEs.
